# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### **Question 1:** Define the MARPOL Convention and list its six Annexes. **[KTU University Exam — June 2024 (Model)]** (3 Marks)

**Model Answer:**

The **International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships (MARPOL 73/78)** is the principal international treaty regulating pollution from commercial shipping. It was adopted in 1973 and modified by the 1978 Protocol, entering into force on 2 November 1983. It establishes rules for the prevention of pollution by:

1. **Annex I** — Oil
2. **Annex II** — Noxious Liquid Substances (NLS) in bulk
3. **Annex III** — Harmful Substances in Packaged Form
4. **Annex IV** — Sewage
5. **Annex V** — Garbage
6. **Annex VI** — Air Pollution

(3 Marks: 1 for definition, 2 for listing)

---

### **Question 2:** What is a "Special Area" under MARPOL? Give any two examples. **[KTU University Exam — Dec 2023 (Model)]** (3 Marks)

**Model Answer:**

A **Special Area** is a sea region identified under MARPOL where, due to its oceanographic and ecological characteristics (such as low water exchange, sensitive ecosystems, or heavy maritime traffic), stricter discharge controls are imposed than the general MARPOL standards.

Examples:
- **Baltic Sea** — Special Area under Annexes I, IV, V, and VI (ECA)
- **Mediterranean Sea** — Special Area under Annexes I, II, and V

(3 Marks: 2 for definition + 1 for examples)

---

## 5.2 PART B — Descriptive Questions (14 Marks Each, with Internal Choice)

### **Question A — Option 1:** Discuss the evolution, structure, and six Annexes of the MARPOL Convention in detail. **[KTU University Exam — June 2024 (Model)]** (14 Marks)

#### Part (a) — Evolution and Structural Framework (7 Marks)

**Model Answer:**

**Historical Evolution:**

MARPOL was conceived following a series of catastrophic oil spills during the 1960s and 1970s. The *Torrey Canyon* (1967), *Amoco Cadiz* (1978), and other incidents demonstrated the inadequacy of existing voluntary measures. The IMO adopted the original **MARPOL Convention in 1973** in London, but its rapid entry into force was stalled by technical complexity. Following the *Amoco Cadiz* disaster, the **1978 MARPOL Protocol** was adopted as a streamlined path to bring the convention into force.

**Entry into Force:**
The combined instrument, **MARPOL 73/78**, entered into force on **2 November 1983**, with Annexes I and II becoming operative on that date. Subsequent annexes entered into force as follows:

| Annex | Subject | Entry into Force |
|---|---|---|
| I | Oil | 2 October 1983 |
| II | NLS | 6 April 1987 |
| III | Packaged Substances | 1 July 1992 |
| IV | Sewage | 27 September 2003 |
| V | Garbage | 31 December 1988 |
| VI | Air Pollution | 19 May 2005 |

**Structural Framework:**

MARPOL employs the **"tacit acceptance"** amendment procedure for technical Annexes, meaning amendments enter into force after a set period unless a specified number of contracting governments object. This allows rapid regulatory adaptation.

**Special Areas Concept:** Enclosed or sensitive seas requiring *zero or near-zero* discharge of certain pollutants.

#### Part (b) — Detailed Analysis of Each Annex (7 Marks)

**Annex I — Oil:**
Discharge limit **15 ppm** of oil in bilge water. Mandatory **Segregated Ballast Tanks (SBT)** for crude oil tankers above 20,000 DWT. **IOPP Certificate** required. Post-*Exxon Valdez* amendments mandated double-hull construction for new tankers.

**Annex II — Noxious Liquid Substances:**
Categorizes bulk chemicals into **X, Y, Z, OS** based on environmental hazard. Discharge only via approved procedures; prewash mandatory for high-category substances. **NLS Certificate** required.

**Annex III — Packaged Harmful Substances:**
Applies the **IMDG Code** standards for marking, labeling, stowage, and documentation. Discharge to sea is prohibited.

**Annex IV — Sewage:**
Ships ≥ 400 GT must have a **sewage treatment plant** or holding tank. Discharge permitted beyond **3 nm** (treated) or **12 nm** (untreated, comminuted).

**Annex V — Garbage:**
**Plastics are absolutely prohibited** from discharge anywhere at sea. Food waste may be discharged beyond **3 nm**. Special Areas impose stricter rules.

**Annex VI — Air Pollution:**
Sets SO$_x$ limits (global 0.50%, ECA 0.10%), NOx Tier standards (I, II, III), and energy efficiency measures (**EEDI, SEEMP, CII**). Aim: IMO net-zero GHG by 2050.

---

### **Question B — Option 2:** Explain the OPRC Convention 1990, the BWM Convention 2004, and the Anti-Fouling Systems Convention 2001. **[KTU University Exam — Dec 2023 (Model)]** (14 Marks)

#### Part (a) — OPRC 1990 and its HNS Extension (7 Marks)

**Model Answer:**

The **International Convention on Oil Pollution Preparedness, Response and Co-operation (OPRC)** was adopted on **30 November 1990** and entered into force on **13 May 1995**. It is a response-oriented treaty, complementing MARPOL's prevention-oriented regime.

**Three-Pillar Architecture:**

1. **Preparedness:** Every oil tanker and any other ship above 150 GT must carry a **Shipboard Oil Pollution Emergency Plan (SOPEP)**, approved by the flag state.
2. **Response:** Coastal states must maintain **national contingency plans**, stockpiled dispersants, response vessels, and trained personnel.
3. **Co-operation:** States pledge to assist one another in spill response, including sharing expertise and equipment.

**OPRC-HNS Protocol (2000):** Extends OPRC to **Hazardous and Noxious Substances** beyond oil — including bulk chemicals. Entered into force **14 June 2007**.

**Significance:** Triggered the establishment of regional agreements such as **HELCOM** (Baltic), **REMPEC** (Mediterranean), and the **Bonn Agreement** (North Sea).

#### Part (b) — BWM 2004 and AFS 2001 (7 Marks)

**Ballast Water Management Convention (BWM):**

Adopted **13 February 2004**, entered into force **8 September 2017**. It addresses the **invasive species transfer** problem caused by ships' ballast water.

**Two Standards:**
- **D-1 (Ballast Water Exchange):** ≥ 95% volumetric exchange in open ocean.
- **D-2 (Biological Discharge Standard):** ≤ 10 organisms ≥ 50 μm/m³; ≤ 10 organisms < 50 μm/mL; specific microbiological indicator limits.

Ships must carry a **BWMP** (Ballast Water Management Plan) and **BWRB** (Record Book). Implementation scheduled in phases, with full compliance by **8 September 2024** for existing vessels.

**Anti-Fouling Systems Convention (AFS 2001):**

Adopted **5 October 2001**, entered into force **17 September 2008**. The treaty bans the use of **organotin compounds** (especially TBT — Tributyltin) in anti-fouling paints due to severe endocrine-disruption effects on marine life (notably *imposex* in sea snails).

**Phase-in:**
- From **1 January 2003**: Application of TBT-based paints banned.
- From **1 January 2008**: Ships must not bear TBT on hulls or external surfaces.

Alternative compliant coatings include copper-based systems, silicone foul-release coatings, and biocide-free surfaces.

---

## 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Student Mistakes That Cost Marks:**
>
> 1. **MARPOL year confusion:** Students often write "MARPOL 73" or "MARPOL 78" alone. Always write **MARPOL 73/78** — both years are part of the official name.
> 2. **Annex entry-into-force dates:** Examiners specifically test these dates. Memorize at least Annex I (1983), Annex V (1988), Annex VI (2005), BWM (2017).
> 3. **Confusing OPRC and OPRC-HNS:** OPRC covers **oil only**; OPRC-HNS Protocol extends it to **Hazardous and Noxious Substances**. Both are distinct legal instruments.
> 4. **D-1 vs. D-2 confusion:** D-1 is the **exchange** standard; D-2 is the **biological quality** standard. A ship can comply with D-1 temporarily but must eventually meet D-2.
> 5. **SOPEP vs. SMPEP:** SOPEP = Oil Pollution Emergency Plan; SMPEP = Marine Pollution Emergency Plan (for chemicals). Both are mandatory but cover different scenarios.
> 6. **CLC formula misuse:** Students often misquote the CLC 92 liability formula. Memorize:
>    - For GT ≤ 5,000: **SDR 4.51 million flat**
>    - For GT > 5,000: **SDR 4.51 million + SDR 631 × (GT − 5,000)**
> 7. **UNCLOS as a stand-alone pollution convention:** UNCLOS is the *umbrella* law; it does NOT replace MARPOL. Examiners expect students to identify UNCLOS as the framework within which IMO conventions operate.
> 8. **Annex VI SO$_x$ limits:** Global limit is **0.50%** since 2020; ECA limit is **0.10%** since 2015. Many students swap these values.

---

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **High-Density Revision Checklist — Module 4: International Maritime Pollution Treaties**

### Foundational Concepts
- ☐ **IMO** = International Maritime Organization (UN specialized agency, London).
- ☐ **MARPOL 73/78** = the single most important pollution treaty; six Annexes.
- ☐ **UNCLOS Part XII** (Articles 192–237) provides the umbrella framework.
- ☐ **Flag state jurisdiction** governs ships; **port state** controls; **coastal state** protects its waters.

### MARPOL Annexes — Must-Know
- ☐ **Annex I**: Oil, **15 ppm**, IOPP Certificate, SBT for > 20,000 DWT tankers.
- ☐ **Annex II**: NLS, Categories **X, Y, Z, OS**, NLS Certificate, P&A Manual.
- ☐ **Annex III**: Packaged substances, IMDG Code compliance.
- ☐ **Annex IV**: Sewage, **3 nm treated / 12 nm untreated**.
- ☐ **Annex V**: Garbage, **plastics banned always**, Special Areas strict.
- ☐ **Annex VI**: Air, SO$_x$ **0.50% global / 0.10% ECA**, NOx Tier I/II/III, EEDI/SEEMP/CII.

### Other Critical Conventions
- ☐ **OPRC 1990** (EIF 1995): Oil spill response; SOPEP mandatory.
- ☐ **OPRC-HNS 2000** (EIF 2007): Extends OPRC to chemicals.
- ☐ **BWM 2004** (EIF 2017): D-1 exchange / D-2 biological; full D-2 by **8 Sept 2024**.
- ☐ **AFS 2001** (EIF 2008): TBT ban — applied from 2003 / not on hull from 2008.
- ☐ **CLC 92** (EIF 1996): Strict liability for oil tankers; SDR formula.
- ☐ **FUND 92** (EIF 1996): Supplementary compensation.
- ☐ **Bunker 2001** (EIF 2008): Liability for non-tanker bunker spills; compulsory insurance.
- ☐ **HNS Convention**: Pending entry into force; covers chemical spills.
- ☐ **London Convention 1972 / Protocol 1996**: Controls ocean dumping (separate from MARPOL).
- ☐ **Intervention Convention 1969**: Coastal state rights on the high seas.

### Key Concepts
- ☐ **Tacit Acceptance** — MARPOL amendment procedure.
- ☐ **Special Areas** — Mediterranean, Baltic, Black, Red, North, Antarctic, Caribbean.
- ☐ **SDR (Special Drawing Rights)** — IMF basket currency used in liability limits.
- ☐ **SOPEP / SMPEP** — Emergency plans for oil / chemicals.
- ☐ **IOPP / IAPP / ISPP** — Certificates under Annexes I, VI, IV respectively.
- ☐ **Three Pillars**: Prevention → Response → Liability.

### Numerical Must-Knows
- ☐ Oil discharge limit: **15 ppm**
- ☐ Sewage: **3 nm treated / 12 nm untreated**
- ☐ SO$_x$ ECA: **0.10% m/m**
- ☐ SO$_x$ global: **0.50% m/m**
- ☐ CLC base liability: **SDR 4.51 million** (≤ 5,000 GT); add **SDR 631 per GT** above 5,000.
- ☐ D-2 biological limit: **≤ 10 organisms ≥ 50 μm/m³** and **≤ 10 organisms < 50 μm/mL**.
- ☐ BWM full compliance deadline (existing ships): **8 September 2024**.

### Exam Strategy
- ☐ Always quote **Annex numbers**, **Article numbers**, and **entry-into-force dates**.
- ☐ Use **tables** wherever comparing conventions.
- ☐ Draw **flow diagrams** to show decision trees (e.g., "Is discharge permitted?").
- ☐ Link each treaty to the **Three Pillars** (Prevention, Response, Liability) for a structured answer.

---
**END OF MODULE 4 NOTES — OESFT727 MARITIME SAFETY**
*Prepared as per KTU 2024 Scheme Board Examination Standards*
